The Moog EP-3 Expression Pedal is a versatile controller designed to provide real-time parameter control for pedals, synthesizers, and other compatible devices. Built with Moog’s signature quality, the EP-3 features a smooth pedal action and adjustable output range for precise control over effect parameters such as delay time, filter frequency, or modulation depth. The pedal includes a polarity switch to ensure compatibility with a wide variety of equipment. Its rugged construction and stable design make it suitable for both studio and live performance environments. Musicians can use the EP-3 to add expressive control to effects and synth setups, enabling dynamic sound shaping during performance.

The Moog MF-101 Low Pass Filter is a classic analog filter pedal derived from Moog’s legendary synthesizer technology. Designed to bring the iconic Moog ladder filter sound to guitarists and bassists, the MF-101 provides rich, warm filtering with powerful resonance and envelope control. The pedal can create smooth tone shaping, funky auto-wah effects, and deep synth-like textures. Controls for cutoff frequency, resonance, envelope amount, and mix allow extensive tonal experimentation. The MF-101 also supports expression pedal control for real-time parameter modulation. Built with high-quality analog components, the pedal delivers the unmistakable sonic character that made Moog filters famous in electronic music and beyond.

The Moog MF-102 Ring Modulator is an analog effects pedal capable of producing a wide range of unique and experimental sounds. Ring modulation multiplies two signals together, generating complex harmonic textures, metallic tones, and synth-like effects. The MF-102 includes controls for carrier frequency, modulation amount, and mix, allowing players to dial in subtle movement or extreme sonic transformations. Built-in LFO modulation expands the creative possibilities even further. Guitarists and sound designers use the MF-102 for everything from subtle harmonic coloration to wild experimental effects. As part of the Moogerfooger series, the pedal is built with premium analog circuitry and rugged construction.

The Moog MF-103 12 Stage Phaser is a premium analog phaser pedal known for its lush modulation and extensive control options. Unlike typical phasers, the MF-103 offers selectable 6-stage and 12-stage phasing, allowing players to choose between subtle movement and deep swirling textures. Built-in LFO modulation provides adjustable rate and depth, while resonance and sweep controls enable dramatic filter sweeps. The pedal also supports external control via expression pedals and control voltage inputs, making it highly versatile for experimental setups. With its warm analog circuitry and Moog ladder filter design elements, the MF-103 produces rich and musical phasing effects ideal for guitar, bass, and synthesizers.

The Moog MF-104M Analog Delay is a high-end bucket-brigade delay pedal designed to deliver rich vintage echoes with modern control features. Known for its warm analog repeats and deep modulation capabilities, the MF-104M offers delay times up to one second while maintaining classic analog character. Built-in modulation allows users to add chorus-like movement and tape-style wobble to delay repeats. Extensive control options include tap tempo, MIDI synchronization, and expression pedal support for real-time manipulation. The pedal’s analog signal path ensures organic tonal warmth, while digital control expands creative flexibility. The Moog MF-104M has become a highly respected delay unit among professional guitarists and producers.

The Moog Minifooger Boost is a compact analog boost pedal designed to add warmth, volume, and subtle harmonic coloration to a guitar signal. Inspired by Moog’s classic analog circuitry, the pedal provides up to 20dB of clean boost while preserving the natural character of the instrument. The tone control allows players to shape high frequencies for brighter or warmer results. The Minifooger Boost can be used to drive amplifiers into natural saturation or to enhance the signal before other pedals in the chain. Its compact design and rugged construction make it an excellent addition to modern pedalboards.

The Moog Minifooger Chorus is an analog chorus pedal that delivers rich modulation with the warm character associated with Moog circuitry. Using bucket-brigade delay technology, the pedal produces lush chorus textures ranging from subtle doubling effects to deep, swirling modulation. Controls for rate, depth, and tone allow players to fine-tune the chorus effect for different musical contexts. The Minifooger Chorus works well for clean guitar parts, ambient textures, and vintage-style modulation sounds. Its compact enclosure and premium components ensure reliability for both studio and live performance.

The Moog Minifooger Delay is a compact analog delay pedal offering warm bucket-brigade echoes and expressive modulation capabilities. Designed with Moog’s analog expertise, the pedal produces organic repeats that gradually degrade in a musical way. Controls for delay time, feedback, and mix allow players to create anything from classic slapback to atmospheric delay textures. Built-in modulation adds subtle movement reminiscent of vintage tape delays. The Minifooger Delay also supports expression pedal control for real-time manipulation of parameters. Its rich analog tone and compact design make it a favorite among guitarists seeking classic delay sounds.

The Moog Minifooger Drive is an analog overdrive pedal designed to deliver warm, dynamic gain inspired by classic tube amplifier tones. Built with Moog’s high-quality analog circuitry, the pedal provides smooth overdrive that responds naturally to playing dynamics. Controls for drive, tone, and level allow guitarists to dial in subtle edge-of-breakup sounds or richer saturated tones. The Minifooger Drive works equally well as a standalone overdrive or as a boost pushing an amplifier into natural distortion. Its compact design and premium build quality make it a reliable choice for both studio and stage.

The Moog Minifooger Flange is an analog flanger pedal capable of producing classic jet-like modulation and experimental sonic textures. Using bucket-brigade circuitry, the pedal delivers warm and organic flanging effects with smooth modulation. Controls for rate, depth, feedback, and mix provide extensive sound-shaping possibilities. The Minifooger Flange can create subtle movement, dramatic sweeps, or extreme resonance effects depending on the settings. Expression pedal compatibility allows real-time control of parameters during performance. Like other Minifooger pedals, it combines Moog analog sound quality with a compact and rugged design.

The Moog Minifooger Ring Modulator is a compact analog pedal designed to generate complex harmonic textures and experimental sounds. Ring modulation multiplies two signals together, producing metallic tones, bell-like overtones, and unusual modulation effects. The Minifooger version provides intuitive controls for carrier frequency and modulation amount, allowing both subtle and extreme sonic transformations. Expression pedal input enables dynamic real-time control during performance. Built with Moog’s renowned analog circuitry, the pedal delivers rich and distinctive sound design possibilities for guitarists and synthesizer users alike.

The Moog Minifooger Tremolo is an analog tremolo pedal that delivers smooth and musical amplitude modulation inspired by classic vintage amplifiers. Controls for rate and depth allow players to shape subtle rhythmic pulsing or dramatic volume swells. The pedal’s analog circuitry ensures warm and natural modulation without digital artifacts. Expression pedal compatibility allows real-time adjustment of tremolo speed or intensity during performance. With its compact design and Moog’s signature sound quality, the Minifooger Tremolo provides classic tremolo tones suitable for a wide range of musical styles.

The MXR '74 Vintage Phase 90 CSP026 is a classic phaser pedal that recreates the iconic 1974 sound. Known for its warm, creamy modulation and smooth sweep, it adds depth and motion to guitar tones. Perfect for classic rock, funk, and psychedelic styles, this pedal delivers a true vintage vibe that has been sought after by musicians for decades.

The MXR 50th Anniversary Phase 90 M101GLD celebrates MXR's heritage with a premium gold finish and authentic vintage phasing tones. It captures the classic '70s sweep and delivers smooth, musical modulation. Ideal for collectors and players who want both a stunning visual and timeless sound, this pedal enhances solos and rhythm parts with rich, dynamic textures.

The MXR A/B Box M196 is a versatile signal routing tool that allows musicians to switch between two amplifiers or outputs seamlessly. Built with rugged construction and true bypass, it ensures reliable signal integrity. Perfect for live performances or studio setups, this pedal provides flexibility and instant switching with no tonal loss.

The MXR Analog Chorus M234 delivers lush, warm modulation with a classic analog character. Featuring intuitive controls for rate, depth, and mix, it adds shimmering textures to guitar and bass tones. Ideal for creating ambient layers, clean washes, or subtle stereo movement, this pedal is a must-have for players seeking rich, expressive soundscapes.

The MXR Bass Auto Q is an envelope-controlled filter that adds dynamic, expressive tonal shaping to bass guitars. Responding to your playing attack, it produces wah-like sweeps and funky textures. With adjustable sensitivity and filter settings, it is perfect for slap, fingerstyle, and grooving bass lines that demand movement and character.

The MXR Bass Chorus Deluxe M83 enhances bass tones with a smooth, rich modulation effect. Combining chorus and vibrato, it maintains low-end clarity while adding depth and dimension. Featuring controls for rate, depth, and blend, it is perfect for studio recording or live performance, giving bass lines a lush, textured presence.

The MXR Bass Compressor M87 provides precise dynamic control for bass players. It evens out volume fluctuations, tightens low-end response, and enhances sustain. With independent attack, release, ratio, and level controls, it is suitable for slap, fingerstyle, or pick playing, making it a reliable studio and stage tool.

The MXR Bass DI+ M80 is a powerful preamp and DI solution for bassists. Offering EQ controls, blendable clean and drive signals, and balanced output, it ensures pristine tone delivery to both amps and recording systems. Perfect for live performance or studio sessions, it provides tonal flexibility and professional-grade sound.

The MXR Bass Distortion M85 delivers heavy, punchy distortion tailored for low-end frequencies. With tone and blend controls, it allows for shaping aggressive overdrive while maintaining bass clarity. Ideal for rock, metal, and aggressive playing styles, this pedal adds grit and character without losing the fundamental bass punch.

The MXR Bass Envelope Filter M82 produces funky, responsive filter effects for bass guitars. Its touch-sensitive controls allow the pedal to react dynamically to picking intensity, delivering wah-like sweeps and expressive grooves. Adjustable Q and sensitivity make it versatile for various genres, from slap to fingerstyle bass.

The MXR Bass Fuzz Deluxe M84 provides deep, thick fuzz tones specifically voiced for bass guitars. Maintaining low-end punch while adding gritty harmonics, it is ideal for classic rock, psychedelic, and experimental sounds. With blend and tone controls, it ensures full-spectrum fuzz that enhances both live and studio performances.

Featuring sub-octave and blend controls, it allows for subtle enhancement or aggressive octave layering. Perfect for live and studio settings, it enhances solos, riffs, and low-end textures.

The MXR Bass Overdrive M89 delivers warm, punchy overdrive tones designed for bass frequencies. With controls for gain, blend, and tone, it preserves the low-end while adding harmonic richness. Suitable for rock, blues, and fusion styles, it enhances both subtle growl and aggressive drive lines.

Featuring EQ controls, clean boost, and balanced output, it allows players to dial in smooth, punchy, or aggressive sounds for both live and studio use. The MXR Bass Synth MB301 transforms bass tones into rich synth textures, offering analog-style envelope-controlled modulation. Perfect for experimental bass lines, it allows for expressive filter sweeps, harmonic layers, and dynamic sound shaping. Ideal for studio work and live performance, it adds a new dimension to your bass palette.

pedalplex_id: MXR Black Label Society Chorus ZW38
seo_description: The MXR Black Label Society Chorus ZW38 delivers thick, lush chorus tones tailored for guitarists. Perfect for creating depth and texture, this pedal is inspired by the signature sounds of Black Label Society’s guitar tones.

pedalplex_id: MXR Blackout Series Bass Envelope Filter M82B

pedalplex_id: MXR Carbon Copy Analog Delay ILOVEDUST ILD169
